summary: Two-story rectangular house with a raised stone brick foundation border, horizontal wooden plank walls with vertical log corner posts, a mid-wall stonebrick belt between floors, rows of small glass windows on each floor, and a dark red (netherbrick) stepped curved roof capped with stone slab trim.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with raised foundation and symmetrical roof ridge
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (front to back) and roughly symmetrical left-right

elements: ground-level raised stonebrick border platform around building
two full stories of horizontal wood plank walls
stonebrick belt/band separating floors and capping walls
rows of small rectangular windows (glass panes) evenly spaced on each floor
vertical log posts at visible corners
stepped/curved netherbrick roof with stone slab edging
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint ~11x9. Place a one-block-high stonebrick border platform around the base (1 block out from wall).
Construct two stacked wooden-plank story walls separated by a horizontal stonebrick belt 1 block tall that wraps the building.
Place vertical log blocks at each external corner spanning both stories aligned with the outer face of planks.
On each story add a continuous row of small 1x1 glass windows spaced with 1-2 planks between; align top and bottom rows horizontally.
Build a curved stepped roof using dark red netherbrick: two symmetrical long slopes meeting at center ridge; cap edges with stone slabs forming a trim/overhang.
uncertainties: Exact block counts for width, depth, and height are estimated from images (could be off by a few blocks).
Material identification for roof and slabs is high-confidence visually but could be alternate dark red wood/brick or stone variants.
Ground surrounding the foundation appears dirt/gravel but exact surface material is uncertain and not specified.
Unknown presence or design of an entrance/door on unseen faces; none visible in provided angles.
